items.title: Man found not guilty of murdering Darwin mother a 'serious risk' to community

children.content: Sheena Fairfield was brutally stabbed to death in the yard of her suburban home in 2022. The man charged with her murder never went to trial due to mental impairment but he could be detained for 20 years.

image.altText: A court sketch of a white man, with auburn curly / wavy long hair, scruffed, looking right, blue shirt.

items.title: Police investigate suspicious death of man found in Alice Springs home

children.content: A 44-year-old man has been found dead in a home in Alice Springs under "suspicious" circumstances, with NT police launching an investigation into the incident.

image.altText: A white street sign in suburbia that reads 'Nicker Cr". Houses and gum trees in background.

items.title: Aboriginal community workers say diphtheria response 'heartbreaking'

children.content: Residents and service providers in the remote community of Yuendumu, where there are reports of dozens of diphtheria cases, say the NT government has not done enough to keep people safe.

articles.title: NT Health confirms only one possible diphtheria-related death amid outbreak

children.content: The Northern Territory health department is refuting an earlier report that a second person has died with diphtheria in the NT, amid Australia's largest outbreak of the disease since records began.

image.altText: Alice Springs hospital exterior

items.title: Air and land search continues for missing 31-year-old man in Arnhem Land

children.content: The NT Police Force says it continues to hold concerns for the welfare of the man, who was last seen early on Friday.

image.altText: Man with afro and short beard has neutral expression on face

items.title: Man, 20, charged over alleged rape of elderly woman in own NT home

children.content: The man was arrested two days after allegedly fleeing the scene in Woodroffe, outside Darwin.

image.altText: Low-angle shot of a square metal sign, blue and white checkers with word 'police', blue cloud sky background

items.title: Meet the groundskeeper breathing life into a regional war cemetery

children.content: Tending to the living to honour the dead — that's what the dedicated groundskeeper of the Adelaide River War Cemetery says has been the pinnacle of his gardening career.

gallery.altText: A white man with thick, long rugged blonde beard, wearing brown wide-brim hait, khaki garden shirt and shorts.

thumbnail.altText: A white man with thick, long rugged blonde beard, wearing brown wide-brim hait, khaki garden shirt and shorts.

items.title: 'Beautiful, kind, caring man' dies in hospital months after alleged bashing

children.content: Friends of Darwin man Martin Wiberg, 48, are remembering him as a "very gentle soul" after he died in hospital this week.

image.altText: Man wearing sunglasses through a hole in a cement wall

items.title: Top End tourism and hospitality businesses seek lifeline amid rising costs

children.content: A popular Darwin restaurant has closed down and the owner of another local hospitality business is asking for COVID-era support measures to be brought back as costs rise and visitor numbers fall.

image.altText: The front door of a suburban restaurant building, padlocked closed.

items.title: ASIO review of past terror cases didn't extend to prior flag on Bondi attackers

children.content: The head of Australia's domestic intelligence agency reveals it reviewed past terrorism investigations in 2024 which did not extend to a re-examination of the soon to be Bondi gunmen because of a resourcing decision.

gallery.altText: Man in suit and glasses behind microphone, grey background

thumbnail.altText: Man in suit and glasses behind microphone, grey background

items.title: Thousands left without power in Melbourne CBD after local outage

children.content: A power company was forced to switch off power to about 10,000 customers in the heart of Melbourne after a burst water main threatened to flood a substation.

image.altText: Power lines and a street light

items.title: 'I did it': Parramatta council official fronts corruption watchdog

children.content: During her third day on the witness stand, Roxanne Thornton told the Independent Commission Against Corruption (ICAC) she was responsible for hiring processes that were "extremely flawed".

image.altText: Roxanne Thornton in a black blazer, purple scarf, green glasses and short cropped ash blonde hair, speaking.

items.title: 'I thought the plane was going down': 10 injured on Cathay Pacific flight

children.content: An Australian passenger aboard a flight from Brisbane to Hong Kong says he thought he was going to die after the aircraft suddenly plunged mid-air.

items.title: Trump says a deal is close. Here are the sticking points still dividing the US and Iran

children.content: A possible US-Iran agreement is taking shape, but the toughest issues are still unsettled, including reopening the Strait of Hormuz, curbing Tehran's nuclear ambitions, and ending regional fighting.

image.altText: A photo of a shipping vessel carrying cargo containers in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: ISIS-linked women and children to return to Australia within days

children.content: A group of women and children linked to Islamic State fighters are expected to arrive home within days after purchasing flights to Australia.

image.altText: A woman in a black niqab walks along a row of tents, with children seen in the distance.

items.title: Magnussen flops at Enhanced Games, finishes last twice

children.content: James Magnussen, wearing a garish golden suit, has finished dead last at the inaugural Enhanced Games event.

gallery.altText: James Magnussen, shirtless, smiles beside a pool with competitors behind him at the Enhanced Games.

thumbnail.altText: James Magnussen, shirtless, smiles beside a pool with competitors behind him at the Enhanced Games.

items.title: Dezi Freeman goaded officers to 'come and get me' before fatal shooting

children.content: The Coroners Court has heard about Dezi Freeman's final moments before a fatal confrontation at his hideout in Victoria's north-east.

image.altText: Screengrab from footage of Dezi Freeman wearing sunglasses and gesturing while speaking with police.

items.title: V'landys to take over as NRL CEO after Abdo quits to join Tennis Australia

children.content: NRL CEO Andrew Abdo causes a shock in the rugby league world by departing mid-season after a highly-successful six-year stint.

image.altText: Andrew Abdo

children.content: Mix up your mince menu with these versatile and affordable meals for the whole family.

children.content: Classic Australian swearwords and insults are becoming less popular with younger generations. What does losing them mean for our culture?

items.title: A movie about mystery-hunting sheep delights this May

children.content: Plus the rom-com of yore returns in Finding Emily, the farmyard mystery that's been delighting audiences, and a gory Hollywood video game adaptation filmed on the Gold Coast.

items.title: Dog-friendly cafe issued warning after customer complaint

children.content: Kellie Hunter opened her cafe in the Adelaide Hills in 2023 as a friendly place for dogs and their owners, but the local council has now put a stop to it after one complaint.

items.title: Man, 23, charged with murder over death of eight-week-old baby

children.content: A 23-year-old man has been charged with murder over the death of an eight-week-old child in suburban Perth.

image.altText: A door of a police car with police tape tied to the side mirror.

items.title: Victoria will not introduce firearm caps or buyback scheme, following review

children.content: There will be no limits introduced to the number of guns a person can own in Victoria, following a rapid review into the state's gun laws.

image.altText: rifles stacked up on a rack

items.title: Committee rejects calls to review greyhound phase-out bill

children.content: A parliamentary committee has rejected a request to review a bill which would phase out Tasmania's greyhound racing industry. It follows new data which shows 19 ex-racing greyhounds have died in Tasmania this year.

image.altText: Greyhounds racing.

items.title: Blues send out SOS as playmaker struggles with injury

children.content: A hamstring complaint for Mitchell Moses puts a scare through the NSW Blues, with Ethan Strange stepping up and Matt Burton called into camp ahead of State of Origin I.

image.altText: Mitchell Moses at NSW Blues training.

items.title: Push for law change that could keep Baden-Clay behind bars for longer

children.content: The possible release of wife killer Gerard Baden-Clay next year could be pushed back by up to a decade under proposed changes to Queensland's parole laws.

items.title: Jury to visit location of Frances Crawford's alleged murder, court hears

children.content: Robert Crawford is charged with the murder of his wife Frances Crawford, 49, who was found dead partially trapped underneath a ride-on lawn mower at their Upper Lockyer property in July, 2024.

image.altText: a seated woman in a pink floral dress, smiling

items.title: Man jailed for fatally amputating man's foot using circular saw

children.content: Kalman Tal's loved ones have told of their distress at how the 66-year-old's leg was amputated in a public park. Farm worker John Yalu has today been sentenced for manslaughter.

image.altText: A selfie image of a man wearing a black top on a tropical farm

items.title: China launches astronauts into space amid new race to Moon

children.content: China has launched three astronauts into space, including one who will stay for a year for a study of the long-term impacts on a human in orbit, ahead of Beijing's goal of a crewed Moon landing by 2030.

image.altText: China's Shenzhou-23 takes off with fire and light

thumbnail.altText: A white space ship launching alongside a vertical control tower, above a bright orange ball of flame and smoke cloud.

items.title: Government bails out Rex's multi-million-dollar debt to regional airports

children.content: Council-owned airports have been bailed out by the federal government to the tune of $4.8 million, $2 million of which was for NSW alone.

image.altText: The outside of an aircraft printed with the letters REX in blue and orange

items.title: Football Australia reeling as staff review reveals 'toxic, chaotic' culture

children.content: Football Australia employees are asked to use three words to describe the culture at the organisation, and the results are damning.

image.altText: Martin Kugeler holds a mic and speaks to the public

items.title: Australia's opening times are world leading and researchers say it's a strength

children.content: Australia has a "distinctive strength" when it comes to early trading, but more knowledge is needed for businesses to fully capitalise on it.
